skill.name: knowledge-base-search
skill.description: Search and locate relevant content within a local knowledge base (files, indices, or PageIndex). Use when you need verifiable citations (file + page/paragraph) to support answers from local sources.
skill.summary: Search local knowledge base files with verifiable source citations
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This is an instruction-based skill for searching local knowledge base files. All 40 static findings (risk score 100/100) are FALSE POSITIVES caused by misinterpretation of markdown code formatting as shell backtick execution and misidentification of search-result example data as credential access. The skill has legitimate risk factors: it instructs AI to execute glob and grep commands for file searching and reads local filesystem content. No malicious intent, no obfuscation, no prompt injection detected. The skill explicitly constrains itself with safety guards: local-only directories, no source modification, no arbitrary script execution, no network access. Safe to publish with documented warnings.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution via glob and grep
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs AI to execute glob and grep commands on the local filesystem for file discovery and content searching. This is a legitimate operation for this knowledge base search skill. Commands are scoped to local directories. The skill includes explicit safety constraints: 'Search only within user-authorized local directories' and 'Do not execute scripts or arbitrary code.' No command injection vectors exist as no user input is directly interpolated into commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands are standard file search operations and are legitimate for this skill. Risk is mitigated by documented safety boundaries and constrained scope.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Read Access
medium_findings.description: The skill reads content from local files and directories to perform searches. This is the intended core functionality. The skill explicitly constrains access to authorized paths and does not modify source content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem read access is legitimate for a knowledge base search tool. The skill explicitly constrains itself to authorized local directories only.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at three locations. No cryptographic operations exist in any scanned file. This is an instruction-based documentation skill (SKILL.md) and a simple reference checklist (references/guide.md). The scanner likely misidentified unrelated text patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code exists in any scanned file. All flagged locations contain non-cryptographic text such as YAML front matter descriptions, CSV example data, JSON audit metadata, and a simple checklist item.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at knowledge-base-search_audit_result_v1.json:225. This location contains JSON audit data from a skill quality evaluation, not any reconnaissance-related code or instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 225 is within a JSON data structure containing dynamic score details from a legacy audit. No reconnaissance intent or system enumeration is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL / Network Access (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a hardcoded URL at SKILL.md:7. This is a source attribution link pointing to the GitHub repository. The skill explicitly states 'Do not access network resources or external APIs' and contains no network communication logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a static source citation in a markdown attribution block. The skill explicitly prohibits network access. This is a standard repository reference, not a network operation.

low_findings.title: Generic API or Secret Keys Access (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Generic API/secret keys' at SKILL.md:81. This line contains example CSV output data showing a hypothetical search result about API key rotation policies. It is not accessing or reading actual environment variables or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 81 is example CSV output demonstrating search results from a credential policy document. No environment variable access or secret reading occurs in this skill.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Combination Pattern (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION: Code execution + Network + Credential access' at multiple:1. This heuristic finding is derived from the individual false positives above. Since all component findings are dismissed as false positives, this combination finding is also a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The combination finding is composed entirely of false positive sub-findings. No actual code execution, network access, or credential access occurs in this skill.

content.user_title: Search local knowledge bases with citations
content.value_statement: Finding specific information in a local knowledge base is slow without the right tools. This skill searches your local files and indices to return precise answers with verifiable source citations including file paths and page numbers.
actual_capabilities: Search local files using glob pattern matching and grep text search
Retrieve precise answers with verifiable file paths and location citations
Filter search results by file type, time range, and tags
Rank search results by relevance using keyword match and proximity scoring
Support multiple index types including filename, content, vector, and PageIndex mapping
Output structured hit lists with snippets, relevance scores, and source locations
limitations: Cannot search remote databases, websites, or external APIs
Cannot modify or edit source documents, only read and search them
Requires local files and indices to be present and accessible
Search quality depends on the structure and completeness of the knowledge base

use_cases.title: Find internal policy and procedure documents
use_cases.description: Search your internal documentation to find specific policies, procedures, or guidelines with exact source locations for compliance and audit purposes.

use_cases.title: Validate claims against source material
use_cases.description: Verify the original wording of a claim by searching local source documents and returning quote-level evidence with file paths and section references.

use_cases.title: Assemble supporting material for reports
use_cases.description: Collect relevant snippets and source references from multiple local documents to support reports, FAQs, or internal knowledge responses.

prompt_templates.title: Simple keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the knowledge base at ./kb/ for information about API key rotation. Return the file path, location, and a short snippet for each matching result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup of a known topic

prompt_templates.title: Filtered search with file type
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ./kb/ for documents about credential management. Only search PDF files. Return the file path, page number, and relevant snippet for each result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted search within specific file formats

prompt_templates.title: Multi-query synonym search with ranking
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to find information about password policies in my knowledge base at ./kb/. Search for these terms: password policy, password requirements, password rules. Also try synonyms like credential policy and access control. Filter by markdown files only. Rank results by relevance and show the top 5 hits with file paths and section references.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-document comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Search across all documents in ./kb/ for information about incident response procedures. Compare how different documents describe the process. For each document, extract the steps, responsible teams, and escalation paths. Identify any differences between documents. Output a comparison table with source citations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare multiple sources on the same topic

output_examples.input: Search the knowledge base for information about API key rotation procedures.
output_examples.output: Found 3 relevant results. 1) kb/security/credential_policy.pdf page 12 states that API keys must be rotated every 90 days with documented procedure. 2) kb/runbooks/api_key_rotation.md section Procedure paragraph 3 provides step-by-step rotation instructions. 3) kb/audit/controls.md heading Key Management paragraph 2 describes verification requirements for rotation evidence.

output_examples.input: Find documents about data retention policies in the ./kb/policies/ directory.
output_examples.output: Found 2 results in ./kb/policies/. In data_retention.md section Retention Periods, customer data is retained for 7 years after account closure. In compliance_archive.pdf page 34, data classification levels with corresponding retention schedules are defined.

best_practices: Use specific keywords and synonyms to improve search accuracy and coverage across the knowledge base
Apply file type and time range filters to narrow results when searching large knowledge bases with many documents
Verify critical search results by checking the source file directly using the provided citation location
anti_patterns: Do not search outside the authorized knowledge base directories to avoid accessing unrelated or sensitive files
Do not use the skill for external network requests or API calls as it only supports local file search operations
Do not modify source documents during search as the skill is designed to only read content, not edit it

faq.question: What file types can this skill search?
faq.answer: The skill supports searching any text-based file formats. Common types include Markdown (.md), PDF (.pdf), and plain text files. Search uses grep for content matching and glob for path discovery.

faq.question: Can this skill search external websites or online databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill only searches local files and directories on your machine. It does not access network resources or external APIs. All searches are confined to authorized local paths.

faq.question: What happens if my index files are missing or corrupted?
faq.answer: The skill falls back to direct file scanning using glob and grep. If scanning is not possible, it reports the limitation and recommends re-indexing before retrying the search.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my source files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is read-only. It searches and retrieves content from your files without making any modifications to the source documents.

faq.question: How does the skill rank search results by relevance?
faq.answer: Results are ranked by a weighted score that considers keyword match strength, term proximity, section importance, and coverage. Higher ranked results contain more relevant and complete information.

faq.question: What citation information does the skill provide?
faq.answer: Each result includes the file path, specific location (page number for PDFs, heading and paragraph for Markdown), a verbatim snippet, and a relevance score.
